text:
# Billing FAQ

**Q: When am I charged?**
A: Monthly subscribers are charged on the same day each month (your billing anniversary date).
Annual subscribers are charged upfront for the full year.

**Q: What happens if my payment fails?**
A: We retry the payment 3 times over 7 days. If all retries fail, your account is suspended
(not deleted). You have 14 days to update your payment method before data is purged.

**Q: Can I change my plan mid-cycle?**
A: Yes. Upgrades are prorated immediately. Downgrades take effect at the next billing cycle.

**Q: Do you offer discounts for startups?**
A: Yes — our Startup Program offers 50% off for 12 months for companies under 3 years old
with less than $1M ARR. Apply at nirvanacloud.io/startups.

**Q: What's included in the free tier?**
A: The free tier includes 1 shared-CPU VM (512 MB RAM), 10 GB storage, and 100 GB
outbound transfer per month. Free tier VMs are paused after 30 days of inactivity.

**Q: How do I get an invoice?**
A: Invoices are automatically generated and emailed after each payment. You can also
download them from Settings → Billing → Invoice History.

**Q: Can I get a discount for paying annually?**
A: Yes. Annual plans are billed at 10 months' cost, giving you 2 months free compared
to monthly billing.

**Q: What currencies do you accept?**
A: We accept USD, EUR, and GBP. All charges appear in your selected currency at checkout.

# Support Escalation Policy

## Support Tiers

### Tier 1 — Community & Self-Service
- Access: All plans
- Channels: Documentation, community forum
- SLA: No guaranteed response time

### Tier 2 — Standard Support
- Access: Pro and Team plans
- Channels: Email, support portal
- SLA: First response within 8 business hours

### Tier 3 — Priority Support
- Access: Business plan and above
- Channels: Email, support portal, Slack Connect
- SLA: First response within 2 business hours, severity P1 within 30 minutes

### Tier 4 — Enterprise Dedicated Support
- Access: Enterprise plan only
- Channels: All Tier 3 + named CSM + phone hotline
- SLA: P1 response within 15 minutes, dedicated on-call engineer

## Escalation Criteria

A ticket is eligible for escalation when:

A ticket is eligible for escalation when:

1. **P1 (Critical)**: Full production outage, data loss risk, or security incident.
2. **P2 (High)**: Significant performance degradation affecting > 20% of workloads.
3. **P3 (Medium)**: Feature unavailable, workaround exists.
4. **Repeat Issue**: Same problem has occurred 3+ times in 30 days.
5. **Customer Requested**: Enterprise customers may request escalation at any time.

## How to Escalate

## How to Escalate

1. In the support portal, set the ticket Priority to **P1** or **P2**.
2. For Enterprise customers, call the hotline: +1-800-NIRVANA.
3. The on-call engineer will be paged within 5 minutes of a P1 ticket being filed.

## Escalation Contacts

- Engineering On-Call: paged automatically via PagerDuty
- Customer Success Manager: assigned for all Enterprise accounts
- VP of Engineering: escalated by CSM for unresolved P1s > 4 hours

# VM Management Guide

## Stopping vs. Deleting a VM

- **Stop**: The VM is powered off. You are NOT charged for compute, but storage and
  reserved IPs continue to incur charges.
- **Delete**: All associated resources are released. This action is irreversible.
  Volumes must be detached before deletion unless you choose to delete them together.

## Resizing a VM

1. Stop the VM.
2. Navigate to **VM Settings → Resize**.
3. Select the new instance type.
4. Restart the VM.

Note: You can resize to a larger instance at any time. Downsizing is subject to
resource availability in the region.

## Snapshots

Snapshots capture the full disk state of a VM at a point in time.

- Creating a snapshot: **VM Actions → Take Snapshot**
- Restoring from a snapshot: **Storage → Snapshots → Restore**
- Snapshots are billed at $0.05/GB/month.

## GPU Instances

GPU instances are available in the following configurations:

GPU instances are available in the following configurations:

- `gpu-small`: 1x NVIDIA A10G, 16 GB VRAM, 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM
- `gpu-medium`: 2x NVIDIA A10G, 32 GB VRAM, 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM
- `gpu-large`: 8x NVIDIA A100, 320 GB VRAM, 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM

GPU instances are ideal for model training, inference serving, and vector database hosting.

## Networking

## Networking

- Each VM gets a private IP in your VPC and an optional public IP.
- Public IPs are static and persist across stop/start cycles.
- Inbound traffic is free. Outbound is charged at $0.09/GB after the first 100 GB/month.

## SSH Key Management

SSH keys must be associated with a VM at creation time. To change the SSH key on an
existing VM, you must stop the VM, take a snapshot, create a new VM from that snapshot,
and add the new key during creation.

## Firewall Rules

## Firewall Rules

Each VM has its own firewall. Default rules block all inbound traffic except port 22 (SSH).
To open additional ports (e.g., 80 for HTTP, 8000 for an API), navigate to
**VM Settings → Firewall → Add Rule**.

# Nirvana Cloud Onboarding Guide

## Welcome

This guide walks you through your first 30 minutes on Nirvana Cloud, from account creation
to launching your first virtual machine.

## Step 1: Create Your Account

1. Visit app.nirvanacloud.io and click **Get Started**.
2. Enter your email address and choose a strong password.
3. Verify your email by clicking the link we send you.
4. Complete your organization profile (name, billing contact).

## Step 2: Add a Payment Method

## Step 2: Add a Payment Method

1. Navigate to **Settings → Billing**.
2. Add a credit card or bank account.
3. For Enterprise customers, contact sales@nirvanacloud.io to set up invoice billing.

## Step 3: Create Your First VM

## Step 3: Create Your First VM

1. From the dashboard, click **+ New VM**.
2. Select a region (us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-south-1).
3. Choose an instance type. For AI workloads, we recommend **GPU-optimized** instances.
4. Select an operating system (Ubuntu 22.04 LTS recommended).
5. Upload your SSH public key or let us generate a key pair.
6. Click **Launch**.

## Step 4: Attach Block Storage

## Step 4: Attach Block Storage

1. Navigate to **Storage → Volumes**.
2. Click **+ Create Volume**.
3. Choose size (minimum 10 GB, maximum 64 TB per volume).
4. Attach the volume to your running VM.
5. SSH into your VM and mount the volume:
   ```
   sudo mkfs.ext4 /dev/vdb
   sudo mount /dev/vdb /data
   ```

## Step 5: Connect via SSH

```
ssh -i ~/.ssh/nirvana_key ubuntu@<your-vm-ip>
```

## Troubleshooting Login Issues

## Troubleshooting Login Issues

- **Forgot password**: Use the "Reset Password" link on the login page.
- **SSH key rejected**: Verify the public key was added before VM creation. Re-adding a key
  requires stopping and recreating the VM.
- **Account locked**: After 5 failed login attempts, accounts are locked for 15 minutes.
  Contact support@nirvanacloud.io to unlock immediately.

## Common First-Day Issues

### "VM stuck in Pending state"

## Common First-Day Issues

### "VM stuck in Pending state"

VMs typically start within 60 seconds. If your VM is pending for more than 5 minutes,
try stopping and restarting it. If the issue persists, open a support ticket.

### "Cannot connect to VM"

1. Verify the VM is in **Running** state.
2. Check that port 22 is open in your firewall rules.
3. Confirm you are using the correct SSH key.

## Getting Help

## Getting Help

- Documentation: docs.nirvanacloud.io
- Support portal: support.nirvanacloud.io
- Status page: status.nirvanacloud.io

# Refund Policy

## Overview

Nirvana Cloud is committed to customer satisfaction. This document outlines our refund
policy for all subscription tiers.

## Monthly Plans

Monthly subscribers may request a full refund within **7 days** of the billing date.
After 7 days, no refunds are issued for the current billing cycle, but you may cancel
to prevent future charges.

## Annual Plans

## Annual Plans

Annual subscribers may request a **prorated refund** within **30 days** of purchase.
The prorated amount is calculated based on unused complete months remaining in the term.

## Enterprise Annual Plans

## Enterprise Annual Plans

Enterprise customers on annual contracts are eligible for a **full refund within 14 days**
of contract signing. Between 14 and 90 days, a **prorated refund** is available, subject
to a 10% early-termination fee. Beyond 90 days, refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis
by the Enterprise Success team.

## How to Request a Refund

## How to Request a Refund

1. Email billing@nirvanacloud.io with your account ID and reason for the refund.
2. The billing team will respond within 2 business days.
3. Approved refunds are credited back within 5–10 business days.

## Non-Refundable Items

- Overage charges for compute and storage usage
- Add-on services consumed in full
- Reserved Instance commitments after the 7-day grace period

## Contact

Questions? Reach out to billing@nirvanacloud.io or open a ticket from your dashboard.

Support Ticket T-001
Date: 2024-11-01
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: Cannot log in after password reset
Description: Customer reset password but login still fails with invalid credentials
Status: resolved
Resolution: Asked customer to clear cache and try incognito. Issue resolved.

Support Ticket T-002
Date: 2024-11-02
Customer Tier: Enterprise
Subject: VM stuck in pending for 20 minutes
Description: Launched a gpu-large instance; still pending after 20 mins
Status: resolved
Resolution: GPU capacity was temporarily exhausted in us-east-1. VM launched after manual intervention.

Support Ticket T-003
Date: 2024-11-03
Customer Tier: Free
Subject: Confused about billing date
Description: Customer asked when they will be charged for the next cycle
Status: resolved
Resolution: Explained billing anniversary date via email.

Support Ticket T-004
Date: 2024-11-04
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: SSH connection refused
Description: Port 22 not accessible after VM restart
Status: resolved
Resolution: Firewall rule for port 22 was missing. Added rule via dashboard.

Support Ticket T-005
Date: 2024-11-05
Customer Tier: Enterprise
Subject: Requesting prorated refund for unused months
Description: Enterprise annual customer wants to cancel and get a refund
Status: open
Resolution: Referred to billing team per enterprise refund policy.

Support Ticket T-006
Date: 2024-11-06
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: Volume not mounting after reboot
Description: /dev/vdb disappears after VM reboot
Status: resolved
Resolution: Added persistent mount entry to /etc/fstab.

Support Ticket T-007
Date: 2024-11-07
Customer Tier: Free
Subject: Account locked after too many login attempts
Description: Customer locked out after 5 failed login attempts
Status: resolved
Resolution: Unlocked account and reset password.

Support Ticket T-008
Date: 2024-11-08
Customer Tier: Business
Subject: High network latency between VMs
Description: Latency between two VMs in same region is 40ms (expected <5ms)
Status: open
Resolution: Investigating. VMs may be on different physical hosts. Considering live migration.

Support Ticket T-009
Date: 2024-11-09
Customer Tier: Enterprise
Subject: GPU instance shows lower VRAM than expected
Description: gpu-small shows 14.5GB VRAM instead of 16GB
Status: resolved
Resolution: 1.5GB is reserved for ECC memory. Updated documentation to clarify.

Support Ticket T-010
Date: 2024-11-10
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: Cannot log in - account shows suspended
Description: Customer payment failed causing account suspension
Status: resolved
Resolution: Customer updated payment method. Account reactivated.

Support Ticket T-011
Date: 2024-11-11
Customer Tier: Free
Subject: VM paused unexpectedly
Description: Free tier VM paused after 30 days of inactivity
Status: resolved
Resolution: Explained free tier inactivity policy. Customer upgraded to Pro.

Support Ticket T-012
Date: 2024-11-12
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: SSH key rejected on new VM
Description: Added SSH key after VM was created; key not working
Status: resolved
Resolution: Explained that SSH keys must be added before VM creation. Guided through VM recreation.

Support Ticket T-013
Date: 2024-11-13
Customer Tier: Business
Subject: Snapshot restore failed
Description: Restoring snapshot results in restore failed error
Status: open
Resolution: Engineering investigating. Likely disk corruption in snapshot.

Support Ticket T-014
Date: 2024-11-14
Customer Tier: Enterprise
Subject: Requesting escalation for repeated login issues
Description: Third time this month customer cannot log in to the dashboard
Status: escalated
Resolution: Escalated to Tier 4. Root cause: SSO misconfiguration on customer IdP.

Support Ticket T-015
Date: 2024-11-15
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: Outbound data transfer charges unexpectedly high
Description: Bill is 3x higher than expected due to data transfer fees
Status: resolved
Resolution: Customer had a misconfigured backup job transferring 2TB/day. Fixed job configuration.

Support Ticket T-016
Date: 2024-11-16
Customer Tier: Free
Subject: Cannot add payment method
Description: Credit card form throws an error on submit
Status: open
Resolution: Investigating payment processor integration. Workaround: try a different browser.

Support Ticket T-017
Date: 2024-11-17
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: Cannot log in - MFA device lost
Description: Customer lost phone with MFA app installed
Status: resolved
Resolution: Verified identity via backup codes. Disabled MFA; customer re-enrolled on new device.

Support Ticket T-018
Date: 2024-11-18
Customer Tier: Enterprise
Subject: Need invoice for annual subscription
Description: Customer accounting team needs PDF invoice for annual payment
Status: resolved
Resolution: Downloaded and sent invoice from billing dashboard.

Support Ticket T-019
Date: 2024-11-19
Customer Tier: Business
Subject: VM resize failed - instance type unavailable
Description: Tried to resize to gpu-medium; option is greyed out in dashboard
Status: resolved
Resolution: gpu-medium temporarily out of stock in eu-west-1. Customer switched to us-east-1.

Support Ticket T-020
Date: 2024-11-20
Customer Tier: Pro
Subject: Cannot log in after email change
Description: Changed account email; now login with new email fails
Status: resolved
Resolution: Auth cache issue. Cleared session tokens. Customer can now log in with new email.
